title: DORA Information Sharing — Indicator Distribution to Subsidiaries and Branches Coverage

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.5 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.5 (ICT risk-management governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.30: DORA Information Sharing — Indicator Distribution to Subsidiaries and Branches Coverage.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.30' running on index=threat sourcetype="ti:bundle_push" (bundle_id, entity_code, pushed_at, record_count), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: HTTP Event Collector from SOAR or threat intel platform
spl:
index=threat sourcetype="ti:bundle_push" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(pushed_at) as last_push, latest(record_count) as records by bundle_id, entity_code
| join type=left entity_code [
    | inputlookup dora_group_entities.csv
    | fields entity_code
    | eval expected=1
  ]
| where isnull(expected)
| table bundle_id, entity_code, last_push
description: Indicators are only valuable when propagated. Measuring which legal entities received each threat feed revision supports defensible evidence that group-wide information sharing obligations were operationally executed.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes threat and sourcetypes ti:bundle_push. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `dora_group_entities.csv` authoritative list; (2) alert when entity misses two consecutive bundles; (3) exclude non-EEA branches if policy dictates; (4) correlate with firewall rule update logs optionally; (5) quarterly attestation by CISO. Indexes required: `index=threat`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ti:bundle_push`.
visualization: Table (missed entities), Heatmap entity x bundle, Single value (coverage %). Key fields: `bundle_id`, `entity_code`, `expected`, `last_push`, `records`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector from SOAR or threat intel platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=threat` `sourcetype="ti:bundle_push"` (bundle_id, entity_code, pushed_at, record_count).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `dora_group_entities.csv` authoritative list; (2) alert when entity misses two consecutive bundles; (3) exclude non-EEA branches if policy dictates; (4) correlate with firewall rule update logs optionally; (5) quarterly attestation by CISO.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=threat sourcetype="ti:bundle_push" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(pushed_at) as last_push, latest(record_count) as records by bundle_id, entity_code
| join type=left entity_code [
    | inputlookup dora_group_entities.csv
    | fields entity_code
    | eval expected=1
  ]
| where isnull(expected)
| table bundle_id, entity_code, last_push
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Information Sharing — Indicator Distribution to Subsidiaries and Branches Coverage** — Indicators are only valuable when propagated. Measuring which legal entities received each threat feed revision supports defensible evidence that group-wide information sharing obligations were operationally executed.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=threat` `sourcetype="ti:bundle_push"` (bundle_id, entity_code, pushed_at, record_count). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector from SOAR or threat intel platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: threat; **sourcetype**: ti:bundle_push. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=threat, sourcetype="ti:bundle_push", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **bundle_id, entity_code**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(expected)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: bundle_id, entity_code, expected, last_push, records.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missed entities), Heatmap entity x bundle, Single value (coverage %).
